title: DX Review
use_when: Use DX Review when developers are a primary user or adoption path and an API, CLI, SDK, docs flow, examples, setup path, error message, integration, or developer onboarding experience needs review.
do_not_use_when: Do not use DX Review for consumer UX, generic documentation, backend architecture alone, QA execution, release coordination, or stakeholder messaging unless developer adoption and integration friction are the core risk.
output_artifact: DX review with developer persona, integration path, friction findings, decision, and handoff
framework_fit: DX Review fits developer-facing products and surfaces where adoption depends on fast setup, clear docs, working examples, understandable errors, trustworthy APIs, and low integration friction.
failure_modes: Reviews technical architecture without walking the developer's actual setup, hello-world, docs, examples, error, and debugging path.
Treats developer-facing docs as generic docs instead of judging integration friction, naming, examples, trust, and time-to-value.
Approves DX without assigning follow-up to docs, engineering, product, QA, release, or comms when developer adoption blockers remain.

examples.prompt: Use $dx-review to audit this API onboarding path for developer experience, setup friction, examples, error messages, and time to hello world.
examples.expected_artifact: DX review with developer persona, integration path, friction findings, decision, and handoff
